content.full_text:
SENT BY:Manatt, Phelps, et al ; 1-11-91 ; 4:44PM ;          2133124224-3272028          ;# 2


                              MANATT, PHELPS & PHILLIPS
                           A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

                                    ATTORNEYS AT LAW

TELEPHONE (213) 688-7000                    11355 WEST OLYMPIC BOULEVARD              WASHINGTON, D.C. OFFICE
   FAX (213) 688-7494                       LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90064             1800 NEW HAMPSHIRE AVE., N.W.
TELEX: 233892                                                                        WASHINGTON, D.C. 20009
CABLE: MAPH LAW                                                                      TEL. (202) 483-6552
                                                                                      FAX (202) 483-1394


                                            January 11, 1991


BY TELECOPY

Jeanne Pritchard
Director, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 610
Sacramento, CA 95814

                Re:  Televised Debate re Los Angeles County
                     Supervisorial Election

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

     This letter will confirm our conversation of January 9,
1991 concerning the televised debate to be staged and presently
scheduled to be broadcast on January 13, 1991 by Community
Television of Southern California dba KCET, with financial support
from Pacific Bell, in connection with the upcoming January 22, 1991
election for the Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors, First
Supervisorial District.

     In our conversation, you advised that the facts and
circumstances of the event -- including without limitation that
Pacific Bell would be providing financial support, that KCET
would be absorbing the remaining costs of staging and broadcasting
the event, and that KCET would be inviting to participate in the
debate some but not all of the candidates in the race -- would
not result in KCET either receiving any political contribution
from Pacific Bell or making any political contribution or
expenditure to any of the candidates participating in the debate.

     Should any matter stated in this letter be incorrect in
any fashion, please inform me of same immediately.

                                        Sincerely yours,

                                        [signature]

                                        Philip B. Recht
                                        Manatt, Phelps & Phillips

PRR/11


01/14/91    09:35    ☎714 724 6045         CITY OF IRVINE                    ☒ 006


                    INSTRUCTIONS FOR PREPARING SCHEDULE E--CONTINUED
                    CANDIDATE ACCOUNT
                    PAYMENTS AND CONTRIBUTIONS (OTHER THAN LOANS) MADE
                    FORM 420

•  Coded or written description of expenditure. If one of the following codes is entered in the "Code" column, the
   "Description of Payment" column may be left blank unless otherwise noted below. If one of the codes does not
   accurately or fully describe the expenditure, leave the "Code" column blank and fully describe the goods or
   services in the "Description of Payment" column.

                              SCHEDULE E CODING CATEGORIES

"C" MONETARY, IN-KIND CONTRIBUTIONS TO    "O" OUTSIDE ADVERTISING. Use "O" for expenditures associated with
CANDIDATES, OFFICEHOLDERS OR COMMITTEES.   production and purchase of advertising and campaign
Use "C" for monetary contributions to         materials such as billboards and other
officeholders or committees. If an expenditure is an in-kind    stickers, pamphlets, etc. (but not campaign paraphernalia such as buttons, bumper
(non-monetary) contribution, enter a "C" in the "Code" column    stickers).
and provide a full description of the goods or services purchased
in the "Description of Payment" column.                           "S" SURVEYS, SIGNATURE, GATHERING, DOOR-TO-DOOR
                                                                  SOLICITATION. Use "S" for expenditures associated with
"I" INDEPENDENT EXPENDITURES (must be described). Enter          surveying voters, gathering signatures, and conducting
"I" in the "Code" column and provide a full description in the   surveys, etc., including payments to signature gatherings for
"Description of Payment" column for expenditures that are        or solicitation drives, and door-to-door canvassing.
independent expenditures to support or oppose a candidate,
officeholder or measure.                                         "F" FUNDRAISING EVENTS. Use "F" for expenditures associated with
                                                                  the holding of a fundraising event, including payments to restaurants,
"L" LITERATURE. Use "L" for expenditures associated with the     hotels or halls, caterers and other food and refreshment vendors,
production, production and distribution of campaign literature and entertainers and other miscellaneous items. (Use "F" for expenditures for printed
printed stationary, including expenditures for mailing lists,    materials distributed at or in connection with fundraising events.)
postage, design, copy and layout, printing and reproduction.

"B" BROADCAST ADVERTISING. Use "B" for expenditures             "G" GENERAL OPERATION AND OVERHEAD. Use "G" for general
associated with the production and purchase of radio and         campaign operating expenses and office overhead, including office
television advertising.                                          rent, utilities, purchase of office equipment and furniture, supplies,
                                                                  postage, telephone, and miscellaneous office expenses. (But not for
"N" NEWSPAPER AND PERIODICAL ADVERTISING. Use "N"               firms for consulting and campaign management services.)
for expenditures associated with the production and purchase of
advertising in newspapers, periodicals and other publications.   "T" TRAVEL, ACCOMMODATIONS, MEALS. Use "T" for expenditures
                                                                  associated with travel within California. (See note in the "Description
                                                                  of Payment" column.)

                                                                  "P" PROFESSIONAL MANAGEMENT AND CONSULTING SERVICES.
                                                                  Use "P" for fees and commissions paid to professional campaign
                                                                  management or consulting firms.

•  Amount of the expenditure or contribution.

   Summarize in the Summary section at the bottom of Schedule E itemized expenditures of $100 or more, and
   non-itemized expenditures of under $100 which are reported as a lump sum. Report in a lump sum on Line 3 all
   interest paid on loans received, Schedule B, Part 2, Column (d). Also report accrued expenses paid this period on
   Line 4.

   NOTES:    Contributions of $100 or more to, and expenditures of $100 or more on behalf of, candidates,
             officeholders or ballot measures must be entered on the Allocation Page, Page 2.

   --  Payments made on loans received, Schedule B, Part 1, are not itemized on Schedule E. They
       are reported in Part 2 of Schedule B.

   --  Payments on accrued expenses (unpaid bills) which were itemized on Schedule F of a previous
       report are not itemized on Schedule D when paid. Enter the lump sum amount of accrued
       expenses paid this period on Line 4 of the Summary section of Schedule E.

   --  The deposit of campaign funds into an interest bearing account, or the purchase of certificates
       of deposit, stock, shares in a municipal bond or any other asset which can be readily converted
       to cash, should not be reported as an expenditure, and the committee's cash on hand should
       not be reduced by the amount of the investment. These amounts should be included in the
       "cash on hand" figure.

   --  If a committee employs an agent or independent contractor, such as an advertising agency or
       a campaign management firm, and the agent or independent contractor makes any
       expenditures of $100 or more on behalf of the committee, such expenditures may be
       reported on a Schedule E-1 in lieu of reporting such expenditures on Schedule E.


JAN 14 '91  8:19                                714 724 6045   PAGE.001
